data_coverage_summary: The UpKeep Public API covers maintenance asset records, asset downtime states, associated parts, work orders, and meter readings, making it suitable for equipment synchronization and condition or usage-driven maintenance workflows.
buyer_guidance: Use UpKeep when you need a maintenance-system API that combines equipment master data with work execution and meter-driven maintenance triggers. It is stronger for CMMS coordination than for high-frequency machine telemetry.
overview: The public UpKeep API documentation exposes asset CRUD routes, asset downtime status management, part assignment to assets, work order creation and update flows, and full meter and meter-reading endpoints. That means the documented surface can provide equipment records and usage-style operational readings while also supporting writeback into maintenance workflows. Asset data is Supported. Telemetry data is Supported. Writeback is Supported.

available_data.description: The published UpKeep docs center on equipment records and the maintenance context attached to them.
data_points: Asset records and asset custom fields
Asset assignments to users, teams, customers, and vendors

available_data.description: The documented work-order and meter routes support maintenance execution plus usage or condition-style readings.
data_points: Work order records and worker assignments

ingest_considerations: The meter endpoints are the closest thing in the published docs to telemetry, so integrations should treat them as operational readings rather than a streaming historian feed.
Because the documented surface is write-capable across assets, work orders, and meters, read and write credentials should be separated where possible.
No exact public machine-readable spec artifact was confirmed during this review, so the documentation URL should remain the evidence anchor until UpKeep exposes a direct JSON or YAML artifact.

source_evidence.evidence_notes: The public UpKeep docs expose asset, part, work-order, and meter sections with concrete REST routes including asset CRUD, downtime statuses, work-order operations, meter reading creation, and meter notifications. No exact public JSON or YAML spec artifact was confirmed during review.
